ead.archdesc:

English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library records
Cornell University. College of Veterinary Medicine. Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library
24-15-3366
3.4 cubic feet. 3.4 cubic feet.
1897-2004. Subject files, accession records, and photographs. Subject files, accession records, and photographs created by the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine. Collection material in English 5129883
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Finkel
Date completed:
August 2004
EAD encoding:
Laura Finkel, 2004
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, July 2018
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
The library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine was founded on September 21, 1897. It was originally named after New York State governor, Roswell P. Flower, but became the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library in 1992 when it was renamed to recognize the generosity of Dr. and Mrs. Isidor I. Sprecher.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Subject files, accession records, and photographs created by the Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library at the Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine.
RELATED MATERIAL
See also the John B. Taylor and Simon Henry Gage correspondence, #24-4-m.1021, for more information about early funding for the library
SERIES LIST
Series I. Subject Files Box: 1 and 2
Series II. Photographs Box: 1
Series III. Accession Records, Requisitions Boxes: 1-3
Cite As:
Cornell University College of Veterinary Medicine Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library Records, # 24-15-3366. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Veterinary libraries. Veterinary medicine -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. College of Veterinary Medicine. Flower-Sprecher Veterinary Library
